title: Updated: Budget airlines ramp up Scottish services

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: New routes added by Wizz Air and easyJet for winter schedule

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Updated: Budget airlines ramp up Scottish services - News Scotland is gaining new budget airline routes with the start of the winter flying schedule.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Wizz Air has started serving Rome and Milan from Glasgow airport while Edinburgh is being connected to Belfast City airport by a new easyJet domestic service. EasyJet also introduced a twice-weekly service between Aberdeen and Paris Charles de Gaulle with one-way fares starting at £29.99.
